summary: Matched nine small 24-byte stage/ground functions, improving GALE01 matched code by 216 bytes. Most matches were simple Ground state mutators: clearing stage-specific x0_b0 bitfields through GET_GROUND, updating Zebes Vec3 coordinates, setting a Zako Generator bitfield, and refining a Kongo mpLib ground callback. The PR also added typed GroundVars entries, a typed mpLib_GroundCallback/mpLib_GroundEnum, and several static headers to separate TU-local declarations/statics from public headers.
what_changed: Replaced placeholder asm stubs with C for grZakoGenerator_801CAEF0, fn_801D13C8, fn_801D7E60, fn_801D94F0, fn_801DA9D8, fn_801DA9F0, fn_801DD65C, fn_801E41B0, and fn_801E6124.
Implemented several 24-byte stage callbacks as simple bitfield clears: GET_GROUND(gobj)->gv.<stage>.x0_b0 = false for Stadium, Zebes, Corneria, Onett, and Big Blue.
Implemented grZakoGenerator_801CAEF0(bool arg0) as lbl_8049F030.xA_b0 = arg0, backed by a new static struct with bitfields at byte +0xA.
Implemented fn_801D7E60 as a typed Kongo ground callback taking Ground* and mpLib_GroundEnum; when arg4 == mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk2 it sets gp->gv.kongo3.xC6 = gp->gv.kongo3.xC8 + 1.
Implemented Zebes coordinate helpers fn_801DA9D8 and fn_801DA9F0, updating gp->gv.zebes.xC.z/y/x from scalar and pointer arguments.
Expanded src/melee/gr/types.h with stage-specific GroundVars structs for Big Blue, Corneria, Stadium, Zebes, Onett, and existing Ice Mountain ordering in Ground::gv.
Changed mpLib_800580C8 and mpLib_804D64C0_t.x24 from a generic void (*)(void*, int) to mpLib_GroundCallback, and added mpLib_GroundEnum in mp/forward.h.
Moved TU-local prototypes and statics for Corneria, Kongo, and Zako Generator into new .static.h headers included before the corresponding public headers.
Refined numerous grcorneria.h prototypes from UNK_RET to bool or typed signatures and removed public declarations for static-local Corneria functions.
smart_moves: Recognized a repeated stage-callback idiom: many 24-byte functions were just GET_GROUND(gobj) followed by clearing a stage-specific x0_b0 bitfield.
Introduced small stage-specific GroundVars structs instead of continuing to access the Ground union through a generic unknown member.
Typed the mpLib callback path so Kongo’s callback could be expressed with real parameters, including mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk2, rather than mismatched void*/int placeholders.
Used static headers for file-local declarations/statics, preserving static linkage while decluttering C files and avoiding public header pollution.
Modeled the Zako Generator global as a padded bitfield struct at the observed offset, enough to match the setter without overclaiming surrounding fields.
decomp_lessons: For small stage callbacks, first check whether the whole function is a Ground_GObj-to-Ground field mutation via GET_GROUND; these often match as compact 24-byte setters.
Recovering the callback type upstream can unlock cleaner matches downstream: mpLib_800580C8’s x24 field needed a real mpLib_GroundCallback to make Kongo’s callback signature plausible.
Ground union progress can be incremental: add only the stage-specific fields and padding needed for known offsets, while leaving unknown bytes as pad or UNK_T.
If a function is static in the TU, keep it out of the public stage header; use a .static.h when many static prototypes or TU-local globals are needed before definitions.
Bitfield naming like x0_b0 and xA_b0 is useful when the exact semantic name is unknown but the byte/bit position is known and important for matching.
Header prototype cleanup matters for matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with bool, void, Ground_GObj*, Vec3*, HSD_JObj*, or callback typedefs helps propagate correct codegen constraints.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific GroundVars structs use names such as grBigBlue_GroundVars, grCorneria_GroundVars, grStadium_GroundVars, grZebes_GroundVars, and grOnett_GroundVars.
Ground union members use lowercase stage names such as gv.bigblue, gv.corneria, gv.stadium, gv.zebes, and gv.onett.
Unknown bitfields retain offset-based names such as x0_b0 and xA_b0.
Unknown enum values in mpLib use mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk0, mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk1, and mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk2.
New callback typedef follows subsystem naming: mpLib_GroundCallback.
Static headers use names like grcorneria.static.h, grkongo.static.h, and grzakogenerator.static.h, with include guards based on GALE01 addresses such as __GALE01_1DCCFC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use GET_GROUND(gobj)->gv.<stage>.x0_b0 = false to match short functions that clear the first bit in a stage GroundVars block.
Represent byte-level bitfields explicitly when the assembly stores or clears a single bit, as in lbl_8049F030.xA_b0.
Preserve exact field offsets with padding comments, e.g. pad_4[0x68 - 0x4] before Corneria x68 and char pad_0[0xA] before Zako Generator xA bitfields.
Use typed callback signatures in structs and function parameters to avoid ad hoc casts and to line up register/argument usage.
For small coordinate update callbacks, infer pointer-vs-scalar parameters from load/store behavior, as seen in Zebes functions assigning from float* y/x and adding float y/z.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increasing by +216 bytes (+0.01%) and listed all nine affected functions as reaching 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other 24-byte stage callbacks that only clear gv.<stage>.x0_b0 through GET_GROUND.
Search for remaining mpLib callbacks still typed as void* or int that could use mpLib_GroundCallback.
Audit Ground::gv union members for other stages that need minimal GroundVars structs with x0_b0 bitfields.
Look for public stage headers that still expose static-local function prototypes and could be split into .static.h.
Investigate whether grKongo_GroundVars3-style structs can be shared among stages as noted by the existing todo.
